CRITICAL: locks::acquire() now uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE to prevent TOCTOU race
between concurrent file lock acquisitions.

MEDIUM: PID cast overflow in agents.rs and locks.rs — replaced unsafe
`pid as i32` with `i32::try_from(pid)` + bounds check.

MEDIUM: process_scanner::truncate() now uses char_indices() to avoid
panicking on multi-byte UTF-8 boundaries.

MEDIUM: Added input length validation on send, register-agent, and
context-set endpoints (agent name ≤128, content ≤64K, key ≤256).

ADR-002 documents all findings. ADR-008 copied from monorepo.

commit.message:
fix: address Dependabot and CodeQL security alerts (#7)

* fix: address Dependabot and CodeQL security alerts

Dependabot:
- Remove stale package-lock.json (leftover from old Nx/npm setup; was
  causing alerts for axios, lodash, flatted, minimatch which are no
  longer dependencies)
- Move vite and rollup from direct dependencies to package.json
  overrides to pin safe minimum versions (vite >=7.3.2, rollup >=4.59.0)
  following bun update to vite@8.0.8 and rollup@4.60.1

CodeQL (js/polynomial-redos):
- Replace .* with [^\n]* in all regexes executed against full log file
  content to prevent cross-line backtracking
- Replace (.+)$ with ([^\n]+)$ in inline patterns applied to captured
  substrings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve all ReDoS vulnerabilities in parser regexes

- Replace [^\n]* with [^{\n]* before \{-delimited boundaries to prevent
  backtracking through literal anchor characters
- Replace \s+([^\n]+)$ with \s+(\S[^\n]*)$ to eliminate overlap between
  \s (space) and [^\n] (space) in capture groups
- Add ^ line anchor with m flag to \[([\d:]+)\] patterns so the timestamp
  bracket pattern cannot ambiguously re-match [digit] sequences caught by
  the following [^\n]* quantifier

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: eliminate remaining ReDoS ambiguity in parser regexes

- Drop the 'with ' literal before \{name: so [^{\n]* is bounded by the
  unique '{' character with no possible overlap (most important fix)
- Replace /in\s+(\S[^\n]*)$/ with lastIndexOf(' in ') string split to
  avoid regex quantifier overlap entirely
- Simplify parseWorlds saveActivityRegex by removing the unneeded
  timestamp prefix that introduced [^\n]*SaveActivity calling ambiguity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use unambiguous separator to prevent SaveActivity calling ReDoS

The funcname between 'SaveActivity calling' and 'with {name:' is always
a single non-space identifier (e.g. zwift_network::save_activity).
Replace [^{\n]* with [^ {\n]+ so the quantifier is bounded by a space
character that cannot appear in the match — eliminating all overlap with
the literal ' with {' that follows and removing the polynomial ambiguity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: chrisjwalk <8442112+chrisjwalk@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use Swift-compatible Date encoding for GT3 push-to-start

Swift's default Codable Date encoding uses timeIntervalSinceReferenceDate
(seconds since Jan 1, 2001), not ISO 8601 strings. The ISO string caused
decoding to fail silently on the device, so push-to-start notifications
were accepted by APNs but never created a Live Activity.

commit.message:
pcie: stop resetting bridge, use firmware's DMA config

Firmware (start4.elf) configures the PCIe bridge with correct BAR2
inbound windows, UBUS remap, SCB_SIZE, and link training. Our bridge
reset was destroying this config, forcing us to reprogram from scratch
— but we couldn't recreate the dual-path DMA translation that both
xHCI (PCIe 0x00) and MACB (PCIe 0x10 via RP1 EP) need.

Now pcie_init() just:
- Verifies firmware left the link up
- Sets outbound ATU window for RP1 MMIO
- Enables bus master
- Dumps firmware's actual BAR2/UBUS/MISC_CTRL config
- Initializes AER

Also adds BAR2 register dump inside macb_init (where UART is live)
to capture the firmware's actual DMA translation config.

Fix notes text not wrapping before button area stretches (#30)

Long notes text was pushing the right-side container wider than the
rating buttons row, instead of word-wrapping within the buttons' width.

The right-side container was a plain `<div>` whose width was determined
by its widest child. When notes exceeded the buttons row width, the
entire container grew.

- **`vote-song-list.tsx`**: Make the right-side container `flex w-min
flex-col items-end` — `w-min` (`width: min-content`) constrains the
column width to the `shrink-0` rating buttons' intrinsic width, forcing
note text to word-wrap within that width
- **`note-editor.tsx`**: Add `self-stretch` to both display and editing
containers so they fill the column width, and add `break-words` to the
note paragraph for safety with very long words

commit.message:
refactor(jetson): migrate from Docker to host-native deployment

NVIDIA does not provide an official JetPack 6 Docker base image with
TRT 10.x + Python 3.10. The existing Dockerfile targets JetPack 5
(TRT 8.5, Python 3.8, CUDA 11.4) which is incompatible with JetPack 6
hardware. TensorRT engines are not portable across TRT versions.

Changes:
- setup_jetson.sh: Remove Docker build/run, add Python/TRT/PyCUDA
  verification, generate host-native systemd service with sandbox
  hardening (NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem=strict, PrivateTmp)
- setup_jetson.sh: Replace world-writable Docker perms (777/666)
  with standard user perms (755/644), add final chown after model
  conversion to fix root-owned files
- Dockerfile: Mark as DEPRECATED with explanation
- requirements.txt: Bump flask 3.1.3, waitress 3.0.2 (host Python 3.10)
- agrikd.service: Update template comments
- app/main.py, app/gui_app.py: Update path resolution comments
- All docs updated: jetson_deployment_guide.md, project_instruction.md,
  build_plan.md, project_context.md, docs/README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add npm install step before npm publish in release workflow (#7473)

The prepare script runs husky, which is a devDependency. Without running
npm install first, husky won't be available and npm publish will fail.

fix: reset terminal to home directory on solution close

Instead of killing the terminal on solution close (leaving a frozen tab),
restart it with the user's home directory. This keeps the terminal
interactive when no solution is loaded. Quick close/reopen is safe:
both handlers are serialized by JoinableTaskFactory (UI thread) and
_processLock (process lifecycle).
